dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S4417 IS: Pechanga Band of Indians Water Rights Settlement Technical Amendments Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Padilla (for himself and Mr. Schiff) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Indian Affairs

form.official-title: To amend the Pechanga Band of Luiseño Mission Indians Water Rights Settlement Act to make technical corrections, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Pechanga Band of Indians Water Rights Settlement Technical Amendments Act.

section.header: Pechanga Band of Indians water rights settlement technical amendments

subsection.text: Section 3403(33) of the Pechanga Band of Luiseño Mission Indians Water Rights Settlement Act (Public Law 114–322; 130 Stat. 1757) is amended—

paragraph.text: in subparagraph (A), by striking means the land and all that follows through the period at the end of the subparagraph and inserting the following:

clause.text: land set aside for the Band by the Executive order dated June 27, 1882;
land set aside for the Band by the Executive order dated January 9, 1907; and
land added to the reservation of the Band on any of the following dates:

paragraph.text: by redesignating subparagraph (B) as subparagraph (C); and
by inserting after subparagraph (A) the following:

clause.text: any contiguous land held in trust by the United States for the Band on the enforceability date; and

subclause.text: located within the Santa Margarita River Watershed; and
placed into trust by the United States for the Band after the enforceability date.

subsection.header: Additional uses of Pechanga Water Quality account
subsection.text: Section 3409(h)(4) of the Pechanga Band of Luiseño Mission Indians Water Rights Settlement Act (Public Law 114–322; 130 Stat. 1769) is amended by striking groundwater desalination activities within the Wolf Valley Basin and inserting activities to address water quality issues within the Wolf Valley Basin.

paragraph.text: In this subsection, the term Pechanga Settlement Agreement has the meaning given that term in section 3403 of the Pechanga Band of Luiseño Mission Indians Water Rights Settlement Act (Public Law 114–322; 130 Stat. 1755).

subparagraph.text: Congress finds that the parties to the Pechanga Settlement Agreement have agreed to amend the Pechanga Settlement Agreement (and any exhibit to the Pechanga Settlement Agreement) as necessary to conform the Pechanga Settlement Agreement (and the exhibits to the Pechanga Settlement Agreement) in accordance with the amendments made by this section.
The Secretary of the Interior shall promptly execute the amendments to the Pechanga Settlement Agreement (or any exhibit to the Pechanga Settlement Agreement) described in subparagraph (A).

paragraph.text: Nothing in this section (or an amendment made by this section) affects—

subparagraph.text: the status of satisfaction of any condition precedent described in section 3407(e) of the Pechanga Band of Luiseño Mission Indians Water Rights Settlement Act (Public Law 114–322; 130 Stat. 1765); or

the validity of the finding published by the Secretary of the Interior pursuant to that section on October 1, 2020, in the notice entitled Statement of Findings: Pechanga Band of Luiseño Mission Indians Water Rights Settlement Act (85 Fed. Reg. 61966) that those conditions precedent were fully satisfied.

paragraph.text: Nothing in this section alters, or requires the alteration of, any water right in existence on the date of enactment of this Act.
